diagrams.title: Complete Graph K₄ Colouring
diagrams.description: Shows K₄ with 4 vertices, all pairwise adjacent, requiring 4 different colours
elements: 4 different colours (red, blue, green, yellow) assigned to each vertex
Each vertex adjacent to all others - no colour reuse
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates χ(Kₙ) = n - complete graphs need n colours

diagrams.title: Bipartite Graph K₃,₃
diagrams.description: Two partitions (left with 3 vertices, right with 3 vertices), all edges between partitions
elements: All 9 edges from left to right
2 colours: one colour for all left vertices, different colour for all right vertices
diagrams.purpose: Shows χ(G) = 2 for all bipartite graphs

diagrams.title: Odd and Even Cycle Comparison
diagrams.description: C₅ (odd cycle) needs 3 colours, C₆ (even cycle) needs 2 colours
elements: C₅: pentagon with vertices coloured A,B,A,B,C in sequence
C₆: hexagon with alternating colours A,B,A,B,A,B
Arrows showing colour pattern around each cycle
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates χ(Cₙ) = 2 for even n, 3 for odd n ≥ 5

diagrams.title: Greedy Colouring Process
diagrams.description: Step-by-step greedy colouring on a sample graph with vertex ordering shown
elements: Shows how colour choice affects final count
diagrams.purpose: Illustrates greedy algorithm dependence on vertex ordering
